findings.evidence: `rg -n "isoOfLinearEquiv|isoToLinearEquiv_isoOfLinearEquiv" code/TauCeti/Algebra/Coalgebra/ComoduleCat.lean` shows the constructor at line 393 and only the one-way roundtrip at lines 452-455.
findings.fix: Add a simp theorem like `isoOfLinearEquiv_isoToLinearEquiv (i : M ≅ N) : isoOfLinearEquiv (isoToLinearEquiv i) (by simpa using i.hom.map_coact) = i`, proved by extensionality on hom and inv.
findings.issue: `isoOfLinearEquiv` only has the roundtrip `isoToLinearEquiv_isoOfLinearEquiv`; there is no characteristic lemma saying that rebuilding an existing comodule iso from `isoToLinearEquiv i` recovers `i`.

summary: The new conversion API has the useful forward constructor and simp lemmas, but it is missing the reverse roundtrip that characterizes `isoOfLinearEquiv` on existing comodule isomorphisms.
